subject: May SIP monthly meeting
deck_filename: SIP Monthly Meeting 20250513 + SIP biz update 20250520 (1).pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find attached monthly SIP meeting doc for our discussion at 9am tmr.

   - Page 1-31 is the draft document for the SIP biz update to CF/TP during
   their CN visits next Tue, for your review
   - Page 31 onwards are additional business updates, including 1) Apr/May
   biz performance; 2) counter Taobao program; 3) regional direct selling
   roll-out

Thank you very much.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - For P&L, I cannot tell if the margins have gotten better/worse
   - Can we also add another slide that shows assortment increase and UE
   improvement over time?

- We should probably indicate when we started our optimizing efforts.
   Think it was Q3’24 right?

Slide 8

   - How did this numbers look like in Q3’24?

Slide 9

   - Probably good to also show the numbers before to show trend



Slide 11

   - I cannot link the 5.7K with the 2.7K in the table
   - What is the reason that the acceptance rate is so low?

Only at 10%
   right now?
- What is the average discount that they give right now?

I wonder if it
   is simpler to design a program for them to join. For eg. Paid Ads program,
   where they pay a fix % for every order instead



Slide 12

   - Is there a view of the markups of the top 5 lanes before, and how it
   has evolved over time?

Slide 16- 18

   - It is not very clear to understand right now
      - What is the UE of our business before?
Including CIR on Ads?

And
      what was ROAS?
And what was it broken down into new/old SKU?
- What is it right now?
- I cannot tell whether CIR and hence UE has gotten better or worse?

Slide 19 – 20

   - Do we really need the view on Slide 19?
- Can we use the view on Slide 20, but have some historic numbers?

- Probably can call out that it was not good, but it has improved



Slide 21

   - Hard to understand, maybe can focus on top 5 lanes?

Slide 24

   - I like to understand the true cost of each line better. Are we paying
   true cost?
Or we mark up as well on the true cost?

- For eg. RR at 3% seems high. Not sure what LM here means



Slide 27

   - What is our coverage of ads (what % of ADO do we do ads on) right now?
- How does this compare with a CB ST seller?

Slide 28

   - How did we arrive at these numbers?
We tested it or?

Slide 29

   - How come not covered?
I thought these would be top SKUs?

Slide 37

   - Who is approving all these COD reopening of lanes right now?
- Have we identified why COD for Local SIP is higher than CNCB yet?
- How is the fraud situation in VN right now?
- Have we addressed Christin’s questions yet?

Slide 38

   - The BWT for some of these lanes are just too long?
- For eg. VNMY, VNPH, is there a reason why?
- For LFF cross selling, are we doing it for other markets as well?

Slide 41

   - Is this MOM growth?
Or growth since Jan?

Slide 42

   - Not quite sure how to read this table



Slide 46

   - This is for furniture alone?

Slide 47

   - Fashion or furniture?
- And what is the full picture?
Including ERP?

It is being shown in bits
   and pieces right now



Slide 49

   - There is fashion and furniture here.. I also do not know what we are
   trying to say..



Slide 50 onwards

   - Did not really read, unsure of content..





*From:* Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Sent:* Tuesday, 13 May 2025 8:21 am
*To:* 'Cuiwei Sun' <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Cc:* 'Jianghong Liu' <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; 'Shuning Wang' <

Thanks.

- We should probably indicate when we started our optimizing efforts.
   Think it was Q3’24 right?

Slide 16- 18

   - It is not very clear to understand right now
      - What is the UE of our business before?
Including CIR on Ads?

And
      what was ROAS?
And what was it broken down into new/old SKU?
- What is it right now?
- I cannot tell whether CIR and hence UE has gotten better or worse?

Slide 19 – 20

   - Do we really need the view on Slide 19?

   true cost?
Or we mark up as well on the true cost?

- For eg. RR at 3% seems high. Not sure what LM here means



Slide 27

   - What is our coverage of ads (what % of ADO do we do ads on) right now?
- How does this compare with a CB ST seller?

Slide 28

   - How did we arrive at these numbers?
We tested it or?

   - How come not covered?
I thought these would be top SKUs?

Hi Ian,

Pls find attached version 2 of biz update doc for your review.

Key changes/new content are:
1. How is SIP UE: page 5-6
2. How has assortment grown from Q3-24: page 10
3. How has pricing markup improved since Q3-24: page 14
4. How is the ads coverage, plan to improve coverage, and plan to answer
the trade-off question on pricing vs ads: page 22-28.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

Hi Ian, please find the version to be used in our meeting at 6.30pm.
Thank you.
Cuiwei

Hi Ian,

Please find attached version 4 for review.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - Can highlight that changes started in Q4’24?

Maybe box it up or
   something?

Slide 22

   - Do not quite understand this, we are PC2 negative when advertising on
   ADO >5, but we will become PC2 positive when advertising on ADO >1?

That
   does not sound right?

Slide 26

   - What is the absolute PC2 difference here?
% is very misleading?

We
   could have made more overall even though the PC2% is lower?

Especially if
   there is growth?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see responses below and version 5 attached.

Slide 4

   -

   Can highlight that changes started in Q4’24? Maybe box it up or
   something?
   -

      Ok. modified title and highlights on slide 4 accordingly

 Slide 22

   -

   Do not quite understand this, we are PC2 negative when advertising on
   ADO >5, but we will become PC2 positive when advertising on ADO >1? That
   does not sound right?
   -

      PC2 negative for March due to other IDSIP lanes cross-subsidizing
      IDMY lane for Raya push: for the total marketing budget for IDSIP, more
      budget is allocated to IDMY vs other IDSIP lanes; thus IDMY PC2 is
      negative, while other lanes positive, overall IDSIP PC2 breakeven
      -

      Updated the slide to show the incremental PC2 vs status quo;
      incremental PC2 comes from higher ads revenue collected by MP
from SIP. SIP
      ads budget is from export market profit passback (BPSF reduction
initiative)

Slide 26

   -

   What is the absolute PC2 difference here? % is very misleading? We could
   have made more overall even though the PC2% is lower? Especially if there
   is growth?
   -

      Added absolute PC2 difference in table
      -

      for case 1 (ads reduction), absolute PC2$ drops because topline drops
      -

      for case 2 (price reduction), absolute PC2$ drops because the growth
      in topline is not able to compensate PC2 drop per order
